name: Operation

phase_0.boundary_condition: The formal type signature and memory allocation limit that constrains the operation domain and codomain

VINIT.justification: Represents the uninitialized memory state before any input data is loaded into the processing registers

TANCH.justification: Closes the operation by enforcing the final output type and preventing further mutation of the result

AFWD.justification: Advances the computational state along the primary execution path toward the target output

AREV.justification: Reverts the state or applies a fallback transformation when a condition fails or an error occurs

CLINK.justification: Chains multiple primitive operations sequentially to form a complex transformation pipeline

IMSCRIB.justification: Establishes a self referential baseline where the input passes through unchanged, serving as the neutral element

FSPLIT.justification: Diverges the execution flow into distinct true and false paths based on a boolean predicate evaluation

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the divergent execution paths into a single unified output stream, recovering the original control flow

EVALT.justification: Marks the successful evaluation of a condition, routing execution into the true branch

EVALF.justification: Marks the failed evaluation of a condition, routing execution into the false branch or error handler

ENGAGR.justification: Holds multiple potential execution states simultaneously without collapsing them, representing non deterministic or parallel evaluation

IFIX.justification: Writes the final computed result to persistent memory, making the operation irreversible and permanently recorded

phase_3.void_description: Uninitialized register state containing arbitrary memory artifacts before operand loading
phase_3.true_description: Valid computational state where all invariants hold and the execution path succeeds
phase_3.false_description: Invalid computational state indicating a type error, failed assertion, or execution halt
phase_3.both_description: Parallel execution state where multiple divergent paths are held in active memory simultaneously

steps.domain_action: Allocate uninitialized operand space in the processing registers
Bind the identity mapping to establish the neutral baseline for transformation
Chain the primary transformation operator with the operand to form the execution pipeline
Fork the execution flow into divergent paths based on the conditional predicate
Evaluate the affirmative condition and route control flow to the true branch
Propagate the forward state transition along the successful execution path
Evaluate the negative condition and route control flow to the false branch
Apply the rollback mechanism or fallback transformation on the failed execution path
Reunite the divergent execution paths into a single unified output stream
Maintain concurrent superposition for unresolved parallel evaluation states
Commit the final computed result to persistent immutable memory storage
Seal the operation boundary by enforcing the final output type signature

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Abstract syntax tree builder and type checker that translates domain intentions into executable instructions
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Shared memory bus and message passing interface for inter component communication
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Register file and persistent heap for state storage and retrieval
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Instruction pointer and control flow graph that determines operation order and timing
phase_5.alfs_store: Standard library of primitive operations and canonical reference implementations

phase_6.cycle_cost: Computational cycles and electrical energy dissipated during logic gate switching
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Uninitialized operand and operator state prior to execution
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Fixed result in persistent memory and cleared intermediate registers
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0: Reversible logical operations conserve information, with entropy increasing only during the irreversible memory commit phase

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Operation
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def operation_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Allocate uninitialized operand space in the processing registers)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Bind the identity mapping to establish the neutral baseline for transformation)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain the primary transformation operator with the operand to form the execut...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Fork the execution flow into divergent paths based on the conditional predicate) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Evaluate the affirmative condition and route control flow to the true branch)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the forward state transition along the successful execution path)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Evaluate the negative condition and route control flow to the false branch)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Apply the rollback mechanism or fallback transformation on the failed executi...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reunite the divergent execution paths into a single unified output stream)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Maintain concurrent superposition for unresolved parallel evaluation states)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Commit the final computed result to persistent immutable memory storage)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the operation boundary by enforcing the final output type signature)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def operation_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (operation_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def operation_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (operation_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem operation_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
